tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue
tools.name: dispatch_triage

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

[{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Move FoundryEnrichmentProcessor agent tags to OnEnd (#57877)

Move gen_ai.agent.name, gen_ai.agent.version, and gen_ai.agent.id from
OnStart to OnEnd so that underlying frameworks (e.g. OpenAI SDK) cannot
overwrite them mid-span. microsoft.foundry.project.id remains in OnStart.

and include overwrite-resistance assertions.

resource.text:
# Release History

## 1.0.0-beta.21 (Unreleased)

### Breaking Changes

- Package renamed from `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Hosting` to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`.
- Namespace changed from `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Hosting` to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`.

### Features Added

- Library-owned hosting foundation via `AgentHostBuilder` (composable builder pattern).
- OpenTelemetry integration with `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore` and OTLP exporter support.
- Health endpoint at `/readiness` for liveness and readiness probes.
- Multi-protocol composition via `AgentHostBuilder.RegisterProtocol()`. Protocol packages provide extension methods (e.g., `AddResponses<T>()`, `AddInvocations<T>()`) built on top of this API.
- Graceful shutdown with configurable drain period.
- Server user-agent `x-platform-server` header on every response with SDK version info.
- `ServerUserAgentRegistry` for protocol packages to register user-agent identity segments.
- `AddAgentServerUserAgent()` and `UseAgentServerUserAgent()` extensions for standalone (Tier 3) setups.
- `FoundryEnvironment` for Azure AI Foundry platform variable resolution.
- Distributed tracing context propagation via request ID baggage.

# Azure AI Agent Server Core library for .NET

Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core is a shared hosting foundation for Azure AI Agent Server packages. It provides a library-owned ASP.NET Core host with built-in OpenTelemetry, health checks, graceful shutdown, and multi-protocol composition — so you can go from `dotnet add package` to a running agent server in minutes.

[Source code][source] | [Package (NuGet)][nuget] | [Product documentation][product_doc]

## Getting started

### Install the package

Install the library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/):

```dotnetcli
dotnet add package Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core --prerelease
```

### Prerequisites

- An [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/)
- [.NET 8](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) or later

### Start a server (recommended)

Use the builder pattern to create a server. Protocol packages provide extension methods (`AddResponses<T>()`, `AddInvocations<T>()`) to register their endpoints:

```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_CreateBuilder
var builder = AgentHost.CreateBuilder();

// Register protocol endpoints (protocol packages provide extension methods).
builder.RegisterProtocol("MyProtocol", endpoints =>
{
    endpoints.MapGet("/hello", () => "Hello from the agent server!");
});

var app = builder.Build();
app.Run();
```

This starts a Kestrel server with OpenTelemetry, a `/readiness` health endpoint, and the `x-platform-server` identity header.

## Key concepts

### AgentHost

The static entry point. `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` returns an `AgentHostBuilder` for composing protocols and configuring the server.

### AgentHostBuilder

Configures the underlying ASP.NET Core host with sensible defaults: Kestrel on the `PORT` environment variable (or 8088), OpenTelemetry traces and metrics, a `/readiness` health endpoint, and `x-platform-server` user-agent header. Protocol packages use `RegisterProtocol()` to add their endpoints — each protocol registers its identity segment with the `ServerUserAgentRegistry`.

### AgentHostApp

The built application. Call `Run()` to start listening. Wraps `WebApplication` with server-specific configuration applied.

### FoundryEnvironment

Reads Azure AI Foundry platform variables (`FOUNDRY_*`, `PORT`, `SSE_KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL`) to resolve agent identity, listening port, and connection strings. Also detects `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` and `APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING` for telemetry configuration. Useful when your agent server runs as a hosted agent in AI Foundry.

### Telemetry

OpenTelemetry is configured automatically via `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore`. The `AgentHostTelemetry` class exposes the shared activity source and meter names (`ResponsesSourceName`, `InvocationsSourceName`, `ResponsesMeterName`, `InvocationsMeterName`) for the Responses and Invocations protocols. OTLP export is enabled when the `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` environment variable is set.

### Health endpoint

A `/readiness` endpoint is registered by default, responding to liveness and readiness probes. It reports healthy as soon as the host finishes starting.

## Examples

You can familiarise yourself with different APIs using [Samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/samples).

## Troubleshooting

### Common errors

- **Port already in use**: The server defaults to port 8088 (or the `PORT` environment variable). If the port is occupied, set `PORT` to another value or configure Kestrel directly via the builder.
- **No protocol registered**: If you use `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` without calling `RegisterProtocol()` (or a protocol extension method), the server will start but will have no protocol endpoints mapped.

### Logging

The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose startup issues.

## Next steps

- [Samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/samples) — Getting started, multi-protocol composition

## Contributing

This project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit <https://cla.microsoft.com>.

When you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (e.g., label, comment). Simply follow the instructions provided by the bot. You will only need to do this once across all repos using our CLA.

This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/). For more information see the [Code of Conduct FAQ](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/) or contact [opencode@microsoft.com](mailto:opencode@microsoft.com) with any additional questions or comments.

<!-- LINKS -->
[source]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/src
[nuget]: https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core
[product_doc]: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/foundry/agents/concepts/hosted-agents

arguments.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57877) by @needuv ## What Changed `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` was updated so that `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` are now stamped in `OnEnd` rather than `OnStart`. This ensures those tags take precedence over any values set by underlying frameworks (e.g. the OpenAI SDK) during the span's lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` continues to be set in `OnStart`. ## Gaps Found - The **Telemetry** section of the README mentions OpenTelemetry integration but does not describe `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` or the specific span attributes it sets. - The README does not document the environment variables that drive agent identity enrichment (`FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME`, `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION`, `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID`) in the context of telemetry. - The README does not explain the `gen_ai.agent.id` derivation (`agentName:agentVersion` when both are present, otherwise `agentName`). - The README does not explain the `OnEnd` override rationale — a key behavioral characteristic for users debugging unexpected span attribute values. - The CHANGELOG for the upcoming `1.0.0-beta.21` release does not mention this behavioral change (tag timing shifted from `OnStart` to `OnEnd`).
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Update CHANGELOG **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/CHANGELOG.md` **Action:** Edit — add a new entry in the `1.0.0-beta.21 (Unreleased)` `### Features Added` block. Add after the existing `FoundryEnvironment` bullet: ```markdown - `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` now stamps `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` in `OnEnd` instead of `OnStart`, ensuring they take precedence over any values set by underlying frameworks (e.g. the OpenAI SDK) during a span's lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` continues to be set in `OnStart`. ``` ### Step 2: Update README Telemetry section **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` **Action:** Edit — expand the existing `### Telemetry` subsection under `## Key concepts` to add the following content after the existing paragraph: ```markdown #### Foundry agent span enrichment `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` is an OpenTelemetry span processor that automatically enriches every span with Foundry agent identity attributes. It is registered automatically when you call `AddAgentHostTelemetry()` — no additional configuration is needed. The following attributes are set on **every** span: | Attribute | Source environment variable | Set in | |---|---|---| | `microsoft.foundry.project.id` | `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID` | `OnStart` | | `gen_ai.agent.name` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME` | `OnEnd` | | `gen_ai.agent.version` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION` | `OnEnd` | | `gen_ai.agent.id` | Derived: `{FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME}:{FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION}` (or `{FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME}` when version is absent) | `OnEnd` | `gen_ai.agent.*` attributes are set in `OnEnd` so that they override any values that an underlying framework (for example the OpenAI SDK) may have stamped during the span's lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` is set in `OnStart` because project membership is always known before any work begins. Only attributes whose corresponding environment variable is set are stamped; unset variables result in the attribute being omitted from the span. ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Start a server) 2. ✅ Key concepts (AgentHost, AgentHostBuilder, AgentHostApp, FoundryEnvironment, Telemetry, Health endpoint) 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing No structural changes are needed; only content additions to the **Telemetry** subsection. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no unexpected README changes 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected for this processor (it is `internal sealed`), but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
arguments.title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document FoundryEnrichmentProcessor span attributes and override behavior
